name: Kriemhild
description: Kriemhild is a central figure in the Nibelungenlied, a Middle High German epic poem. Initially portrayed as a beautiful and virtuous princess of Burgundy, sister to Gunther, Gernot, and Giselher, her life takes a tragic turn after her marriage to the hero Siegfried. Siegfried's murder, orchestrated by Hagen at the behest of Gunther to steal Siegfried's treasure, the Nibelungen hoard, sets in motion Kriemhild's consuming quest for vengeance. Her grief and desire for retribution transform her from a gentle maiden into a ruthless queen. She marries Etzel (Attila the Hun) to gain power and allies, ultimately luring the Burgundians to Etzel's court under false pretenses. This culminates in a bloody massacre where she engineers the deaths of her own brothers and Hagen, fueled by her unwavering determination to avenge Siegfried. Kriemhild's story is a powerful exploration of love, loss, betrayal, and the destructive consequences of revenge, highlighting the devastating impact of unchecked grief and the corruption of power. She is a complex character, both a victim and a perpetrator of violence, leaving a lasting impression as one of the most formidable and tragic figures in Germanic mythology.
subtitle: The Vengeful Queen of the Nibelungenlied
achievements: Orchestrated the downfall of the Burgundians

keyMyths.title: Siegfried's Death
keyMyths.description: Siegfried is murdered by Hagen during a hunt, leaving Kriemhild devastated and vowing revenge. This act is the catalyst for her transformation and sets the stage for the Nibelungenlied's tragic climax.

keyMyths.title: Kriemhild's Marriage to Etzel
keyMyths.description: Years after Siegfried's death, Kriemhild marries Etzel, the powerful Hunnic king, to gain influence and build an army. She uses her position to plot her revenge against the Burgundians.

keyMyths.title: The Massacre at Etzel's Court
keyMyths.description: Kriemhild invites her brothers and Hagen to Etzel's court, where she orchestrates a bloody battle that results in their deaths. She personally kills Hagen, finally avenging Siegfried's murder.

sources.author: Anonymous
